report_name: GodDamn Ransomware Deploys Microsoft-Signed 'PoisonX' Driver to Blindside EDR

threat_actors.name: Hyadina
threat_actors.description: A threat actor responsible for the GodDamn ransomware, which is a rebrand of the Beast and Monster ransomware families. Hyadina uses a custom, Microsoft-signed malicious kernel driver named 'PoisonX' to disable endpoint security products.

attack_patterns.name: Impair Defenses: Disable or Modify Tools
attack_patterns.description: The malicious kernel driver 'PoisonX' (g11.sys) was used to terminate endpoint detection and response (EDR) processes, such as CrowdStrike Falcon, from the kernel level.

attack_patterns.name: Remote Access Software
attack_patterns.description: The attackers used AnyDesk for remote access and persistence, often installing it in non-standard locations to evade detection.

attack_patterns.name: OS Credential Dumping
attack_patterns.description: A toolkit based on NirSoft utilities was deployed to perform credential theft from web browsers, Windows Credential Manager, VNC sessions, and email clients.

attack_patterns.name: Data Encrypted for Impact
attack_patterns.description: After disabling security defenses, the GodDamn ransomware encrypted files across the system and network shares, appending extensions like .God8Damn.

attack_patterns.name: Create or Modify System Process: Windows Service
attack_patterns.description: The attackers created a new kernel service to load the malicious 'PoisonX' driver (g11.sys), indicated by hunting hints for 'sc create ... type= kernel' commands.

malware.name: GodDamn Ransomware
malware.description: A new ransomware strain identified as a rebrand of the Beast and Monster families. It uses a signed malicious driver, PoisonX, to disable EDR products before encryption.

malware.name: Beast
malware.description: An earlier ransomware family developed by the Hyadina threat actor, which evolved into GodDamn ransomware.

malware.name: Monster
malware.description: An earlier ransomware family developed by the Hyadina threat actor, which evolved into GodDamn ransomware.

malware.name: PoisonX
malware.description: A custom, malicious kernel driver (g11.sys) that was legitimately signed by Microsoft. It is used by GodDamn ransomware to terminate EDR and antivirus processes from the kernel level via IOCTL requests.

tools.name: AnyDesk
tools.description: Used by the Hyadina threat actor for remote access and persistence, often installed in non-standard locations like C:\Users\Public\ to avoid detection.

tools.name: NirSoft utilities
tools.description: A toolkit based on NirSoft utilities was used to perform extensive credential theft from various sources on the compromised system.

identities.name: Symantec
identities.description: The security research organization that discovered and analyzed the GodDamn ransomware operation.

identities.name: Microsoft
identities.description: Microsoft's driver signing program was abused by the Hyadina threat actor to obtain a legitimate signature for the malicious PoisonX kernel driver.

identities.name: CrowdStrike
identities.description: CrowdStrike Falcon was mentioned as an example of an EDR product targeted for termination by the PoisonX driver.

indicators.name: PoisonX Driver File Detection
indicators.x_netsecio_sigma_rule:
title: GodDamn Ransomware PoisonX Driver File
id: 7c8b1a2d-3e4f-4a5b-8c9d-0e1f2a3b4c5d
status: experimental
description: Detects the presence of the g11.sys driver, known as PoisonX, used by GodDamn ransomware to disable security products.
references:
  - https://www.security.com/blog-post/goddamn-ransomware-beast-rebrand
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-07-09
tags:
  - attack.t1562.001
  - attack.defense_evasion
level: critical
logsource:
  category: file_event
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    TargetFilename|endswith: '\g11.sys'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects the creation or presence of the g11.sys file, which is the malicious PoisonX driver used by GodDamn ransomware.

indicators.name: PoisonX Kernel Driver Service Creation
indicators.pattern:
title: PoisonX Kernel Driver Service Creation
id: 8d9c2b3e-4f5a-4b6c-9d0e-1f2a3b4c5d6e
status: experimental
description: Detects the command line used to create a kernel service, a technique used by GodDamn ransomware to load the PoisonX driver.
references:
  - https://www.security.com/blog-post/goddamn-ransomware-beast-rebrand
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-07-09
tags:
  - attack.t1543.003
  - attack.persistence
  - attack.privilege_escalation
level: high
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith: '\sc.exe'
    CommandLine|contains|all:
      - 'create'
      - 'type= kernel'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects the use of 'sc.exe' to create a new kernel-mode service, a behavior associated with loading the PoisonX driver.

indicators.name: Anomalous AnyDesk Execution Path
indicators.pattern:
title: Anomalous AnyDesk Execution Path
id: 9e0d3c4f-5a6b-4c7d-0e1f-2a3b4c5d6e7f
status: experimental
description: Detects AnyDesk running from non-standard paths, such as C:\Users\Public\, which is a TTP associated with the Hyadina threat actor.
references:
  - https://www.security.com/blog-post/goddamn-ransomware-beast-rebrand
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-07-09
tags:
  - attack.t1219
  - attack.command_and_control
level: medium
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith: '\AnyDesk.exe'
  filter_legit:
    Image|startswith:
      - 'C:\Program Files (x86)\AnyDesk\'
      - 'C:\Program Files\AnyDesk\'
  condition: selection and not filter_legit
indicators.description: Detects AnyDesk being executed from unusual locations, a technique used by attackers to hide their remote access tools.

relationships.target_name: Impair Defenses: Disable or Modify Tools
Create or Modify System Process: Windows Service
Create or Modify System Process: Windows Service
